user_message: Before my call with Litware Inc, prep a brief. This is a renewal account with declining consumption.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Sarah Wilson <sarah.wilson@litwareinc.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-04 13:32 EST
Subject: Follow-up: renewal risk Review

Just checking in on the renewal risk proposal. Our VP Operations is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Sarah Wilson <sarah.wilson@litwareinc.com>
Date: 2024-02-21 17:32 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Litware Inc renewal risk

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about renewal risk. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Sarah Wilson <sarah.wilson@litwareinc.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-21 15:28 EST
Subject: Follow-up: renewal risk Review

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about renewal risk. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

Meeting: renewal risk Discussion - Litware Inc
Date: 2024-01-01
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc), David Chen (Microsoft), Kevin (Litware Inc)

[00:01:30] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): Thanks everyone for joining. Let's dive into renewal risk.

[00:08:00] Kevin (Litware Inc): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is performance requirements.

[00:13:00] Kevin (Litware Inc): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is technical validation.

[00:17:00] Kevin (Litware Inc): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is partner engagement.

[00:22:00] David Chen (Microsoft): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about data residency. Can we address those?

[00:25:00] David Chen (Microsoft): On the renewal risk front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around security compliance.

[00:29:00] Kevin (Litware Inc): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is stakeholder alignment.

[00:31:00] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about technical validation. Can we address those?

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF11-M trap (Brief format doesn't natively differentiate first-meeting vs recurring); produces complete One-page brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF11-M failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF11-M failure mode (Brief format doesn't natively differentiate first-meeting vs recurring)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct renewal risk decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF11-M -- Brief format doesn't natively differentiate first-meeting vs recurring
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
